business.headline: Goldman Sachs cuts India growth forecast to 5.9%, flags rate hike risksThe revision follows changes in Goldman's assumptions on oil prices and supply disruptions. Higher crude prices remain a major risk for India, which relies heavily on imports.

8th Pay Commission: When will your salary rise and how much can you getCentral government employees are eagerly waiting for the 8th Pay Commission update. While a decent salary hike is expected, the bigger question remains, i.e., when will the revised pay actually start coming in?

Swiggy hikes platform fee to Rs 17.58 after Zomato, food orders get costlierFood delivery bills are inching up again. Swiggy has increased its platform fee soon after Zomato, meaning customers will now pay more each time they order online.

Bought gold during Iran war? Here's why it's not giving you returnsGold and silver prices have fallen despite ongoing war tensions, which is unusual as precious metals are typically seen as a safe investment during crises like war.

Kotak Mahindra Bank shares rise: Why is the stock gaining today?The sharp rise reflects a recovery after recent weakness, but investors should watch for consistency in momentum as broader market trends and foreign investor flows continue to influence banking stocks.

Gold falls Rs 2,500, silver down Rs 8,000: Why are safe-haven assets tumbling?Gold and silver prices are falling even as the West Asia conflict continues. Here's why safe-haven assets are losing appeal and what it means for investors.

Sensex rallies 1,500 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Today's gains on Dalal Street are being driven by easing West Asia tensions, softer crude prices and strong buying in banking stocks, as investors return to beaten-down large caps after Monday's sharp sell-off.
Sensex, Nifty jump as US delays Iran grid strike; IndiGo up 3%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 993.75 points to 73,690.14, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 319.20 points to 22,831.85 as of 9:29 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening today: Will stock markets crash again amid Iran war?Early signals show a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,878 at around 8:40 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open about 1.6% higher compared to Monday's close of 22,512.65.

$580 million bets before Trump's Iran remark. Coincidence or insider trading?Perfectly timed oil and equity trades worth $580 million before US President Donald Trump claimed "productive talks" with Iran and paused strikes, later denied by Tehran, are raising insider trading concerns.

IndiGo appoints former Air India Express CEO Aloke Singh as Strategy ChiefBetween December 3 and 5, 2025, IndiGo cancelled 2,507 flights and delayed 1,852 others, affecting over three lakh passengers nationwide. The disruptions were linked to challenges in implementing revised Flight Duty Time Limitation (FDTL) norms for pilots.

Wall Street rallies as Trump delays Iran strikesThe positive sentiment followed Trump's statement that the US would postpone planned strikes on Iranian power plants and energy infrastructure for five days.

Sebi eases trade rules for foreign investors amid rising market outflowsUntil now, investors had to settle each buy and sell trade separately. This meant higher funding needs and more complex transactions. With net settlement, they can offset buy and sell positions, reducing the amount of money required for each cycle.
Oil prices fall over 13% as Trump pauses Iran energy strikesOil prices jumped in the morning with Brent nearing $113, but later fell to around $100.34, down 6.2%, later in the day.

CoinDCX co-founders in custody over fraud claims, company blames fake operatorsThe case is based on a complaint filed by a 42-year-old insurance advisor. He alleged that he was cheated of Rs 71.6 lakh between August 2025 and March 2026.
Sensex ends 1,836 points lower, Nifty below 22,600; Titan down 7%The S&P BSE Sensex lost 1,836 points to close at 72,696.39, while the NSE Nifty50 tanked 601.85 points to end at 22,512.65.

Gold crashes Rs 10,000 in a day, silver down Rs 18,000: Should you buy now?Precious metals have taken a hit, with gold and silver prices falling sharply. While corrections are not unusual, investors are now wondering — is this a buying opportunity or a signal to stay on the sidelines?

Share market crash: Why are IT stocks shining when Dalal Street is in red?A look at Sensex stocks shows that IT names such as HCLTech, Tech Mahindra, and TCS were trading in the green, even as heavyweights across banking, auto, metal and consumption sectors saw sharp declines.

Your iPhone's India-made: Fareed Zakaria's US lecture highlights India pipping ChinaSmartphone shipments from India became its top export while it displaced China as the biggest supplier of iPhones to the US. The feat is being lauded yet again with analyst Fareed Zakaria highlighting it in his lecture. Where does India stand vis a vis China in the smartphones game?

Rupee in free fall: Will it hit Rs 95 against the US dollar?The currency weakened to around Rs 93.9 against the US dollar on Monday, marking its weakest level ever and bringing the Rs 94–95 range into focus.

entertainment.headline: Rashmika-Vijay's wedding planner shares note, calls them super creative couple Wedding planner Priya Maganti shared insights into Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda's intimate wedding, calling it a deeply personal and emotional experience.

Miley Cyrus sparkles as Hannah Montana at premiere, poses with fiance Maxx Morando Miley Cyrus walked the Hannah Montana anniversary special red carpet with fiance Maxx Morando and family in Hollywood. The special, Hannahversary, is set to premiere online on March 24.

RGV compares Dhurandhar 2 with War 2 in new review of Aditya Dhar's film Ram Gopal Varma broke down Dhurandhar: The Revenge, comparing it to big-budget action films and highlighting its emotional realism. He explained why Ranveer Singh's performance as Hamza feels more human than typical 'superhero' portrayals.

Actor Naveen Kaushik reacts after Dhruv Rathee calls Dhurandhar 2 brain rot Naveen Kaushik responded to Dhruv Rathee's criticism of Dhurandhar 2. He urged audiences not to turn the film into a source of hate despite differing opinions.

Reacher star Alan Ritchson accused of assaulting neighbour over bike noise Actor Alan Ritchson is facing scrutiny after his neighbour alleged a violent altercation over dirt bike noise in Tennessee. His neighbour, Ronnie Taylor, claimed in a recent interview that he was punched by him multiple times.

Dhurandhar 2: Aditya Dhar gave Rakesh Bedi his best comic role ever, with a catch Rakesh Bedi's Jameel Jamali in Dhurandhar: The Revenge steals every frame with comic expressions and quirky dialogues, providing relief amid high-octane drama and gore. The actor's job, though, runs deeper in the script.

Superstar moment: Aditya Dhar reacts as Rajinikanth calls Dhurandhar 2 a must-watch Filmmaker Aditya Dhar shared an emotional note after Rajinikanth called Dhurandhar 2 a must-watch. The action-thriller, starring Ranveer Singh, Arjun Rampal, R Madhavan, Rakesh Bedi and Sanjay Dutt among others, follows Hamza's mission against terrorism.

Is Dhurandhar 3 happening? Mukesh Chhabra shares update on Ranveer Singh's film Casting director Mukesh Chhabra put an end to Dhurandhar 3 rumours amid fan speculation. The rumours of a third instalment to the hit action thriller grew after a fake end-credit clip and director Aditya Dhar's post on end credits.

Dhurandhar 2: Ranveer Singh's best moments amid gore are ones that let him cry Ranveer Singh delivered a career-defining performance as an action hero in Dhurandhar: The Revenge. Four powerful scenes highlighted the versatile actor's unmatched emotional depth.

Valerie Perrine dies at 82, fans remember Superman actor's legacy Veteran actor Valerie Perrine has died at 82 after a long battle with Parkinson's disease. She was known for her roles in Lenny and the Superman films.

Hey Jude: Charlie Puth, wife Brooke Sansone welcome baby boy; share pics Singer-songwriter Charlie Puth and his wife, Brooke Sansone, welcomed their first child. The couple shared heartfelt glimpses of baby Jude on Instagram.

Dhurandhar 2 box office Day 5 crushes Pushpa 2 Hindi, Rs 800 crore worldwide Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge has entered the Rs 500 crore club domestically in five days, and has surpassed the Rs 800 crore mark worldwide. It has also outpaced Allu Arjun's Pushpa 2: The Role in the Hindi market. Check the latest box office collection report here.

Moana trailer unveils Dwayne Johnson's live-action Maui. Check out release date Disney has released the live-action Moana trailer, introducing Dwayne Johnson as Maui. Here's what the first look reveals about the film.

Box office ka baap: Rajinikanth hails Aditya Dhar after Dhurandhar 2 success Rajinikanth's praise for Dhurandhar 2 is making headlines as the film dominates the box office. Here's what he said about Aditya Dhar.

Iran premieres Night Light as response to Israeli show Tehran amid war Iran's new series Night Light is drawing comparisons with Tehran, but with a different perspective. Here's why its launch is making headlines.

AI reimagines Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 ending, fans react With Dhurandhar: The Revenge witnessing strong box office numbers, the film's emotional climax has gone viral, with fans using AI to reimagine Jaskirat's ending.

Dhurandhar 2 pushes Project Hail Mary India release, director reacts: How dare you? Project Hail Mary co-directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller reacted to the sci-fi film's India release being rescheduled. The film was earlier expected to clash with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge.

Drishyam 3 delayed as Georgekutty plans comeback on Mohanlal's birthday The highly anticipated Malayalam film Drishyam 3 has a new release date and is now set to arrive on Mohanlal's birthday. Here's what the delay means and why fans are excited.

Thaai Kizhavi box office: Radhika Sarathkumar film crosses Rs 75 crore mark Thaai Kizhavi crosses Rs 75 crore worldwide in 25 days, as Radhika Sarathkumar's film wins praise for blending humour with meaningful social themes

general news.headline: Only Hindus, Sikhs and Buddhists can claim Scheduled Castes status: Supreme CourtIndividuals professing Hinduism, Sikhism or Buddhism are eligible for Scheduled Caste (SC) status, but if one converts to any other religion, it will result in the person losing SC status, the Supreme Court said.

You said, 'Let's do it': Trump shifts Iran war blame to Pete HegsethAs the Iran war drags on, Trump appears to rewrite how it began, pointing at his defence chief. But warnings, denials and shifting claims suggest a story far messier than he lets on.

Clerical error: Election Commission after letter with Kerala BJP seal sparks rowA political row erupted after an Election Commission letter with a BJP Kerala unit's seal surfaced online. The poll panel called the lapse a "clerical error," saying it was fixed immediately, and suspended an Assistant Section Officer over the incident.

Government to fast-track women's quota, Parliament session likely to be extendedThe ongoing Budget Session of Parliament may be extended beyond April 2. According to sources, the government is considering a dedicated two-day session to push through amendments to the Women's Reservation Act.

Understanding Major Iqbal: Why Dhurandhar's chilling villain loses edge by climaxArjun Rampal's Major Iqbal is equal parts menacing and vulnerable. Aditya Dhar's Dhurandhar: The Revenge gives him the kind of arc that breaks the usual Bollywood template of a villain, and yet just when it reaches a high, the film does something unthinkable.

Live: Rajnath Singh meets service chiefs, reviews security amid West Asia crisisUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East conflict is in its fourth week with no let-up, even as tentative signs of de-escalation emerge alongside continued military posturing. Iran launched multiple waves of missiles at Israel on Monday, setting off air raid sirens in Tel Aviv and beyond, even as US President Donald Trump delayed a planned strike on Iranian energy infrastructure citing "productive" talks with Tehran. The attacks, which caused damage to homes in northern Israel but no reported deaths, came amid heightened tensions after Iran moved to effectively shut the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global energy route. Meanwhile, at a roundtable in Tennessee, Trump suggested Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth was the first to push for military action. Stay with IndiaToday.in for live updates on the Middle East crisis.

Iran trolls Trump's ego. Why it may prove to be costlyAfter Trump announced a five-day halt in strikes on Iran's power infrastructure, Tehran did not respond with restraint. Instead, it went all out mocking Trump, framing it as a sign of Trump's weakness. However, history has shown that an egotistical Trump tends to react strongly to such slights. Is Iran playing with fire?

Memes, missiles and mockery: How Iran is winning the battle against TrumpIran isn't just fighting the US and Israel just with missiles and drones. It is flooding the internet with memes and AI-generated videos, mocking President Donald Trump. There are videos featuring Trump and other leaders as Lego and Teletubbies-style characters. In one video, Iran declares itself the 'Lord of the Strait'.

Claude can now control computer like a humanClaude can now get full access to your computer, do tasks by itself
Anthropic has launched a new feature for Claude that allows the AI to get complete access to your computer. The AI can then complete tasks on your behalf while you are away. This feature comes just days after the company launched "Dispatch" which allows you to control Claude via your phone.

Egypt, Turkey to India, Saudi Arabia: How countries opened US-Iran back channelEgypt has emerged as a key back-channel mediator, establishing contact with Iran's IRGC and proposing a five-day pause in fighting to build momentum for a ceasefire, a move that appears to have nudged US President Donald Trump to drop his threat to strike Iran's power plants, The Wall Street Journal reported.

Denial of Permanent Commission to women officers due to systemic discrimination: SCThe Supreme Court granted Permanent Commission to women SSC officers, striking down systemic discrimination, the arbitrary 250-officer cap, and unfair evaluation practices in the armed forces.

Seven dogs escape meat traders in China, trek 17 km home in heartwarming videoSeven stolen village dogs escaped suspected meat traders and were filmed travelling together along a busy highway in China.

Did best you could: LaGuardia air traffic controller to colleague after collisionLaGuardia collision: In the recording, the distraught air traffic controller admits that he "messed up". His colleague tries to comfort him, telling him he "did the best you could".

sports.headline: IPL 2026: Matthew Hayden picks GT star as heir to his iconic Mongoose bat IPL 2026: Gujarat Titans batting coach Matthew Hayden named Rahul Tewatia as the successor to his iconic Mongoose bat, praising the GT finisher's power-hitting ahead of IPL 2026.

KKR's injury crisis deepens, Akash Deep ruled out for the season IPL: Kolkata Knight Riders have been hit with yet another injury to a fast bowler. India pacer Akash Deep has been ruled out due to a lumbar stress injury.

Is this the make or break season for Hardik Pandya, the MI captain? IPL: Hardik Pandya's return to Mumbai Indians has not borne the results that it was supposed to. Can Hardik get out of Rohit Sharma and Suryakumar Yadav's shadow this season.

If Maradona was God at Napoli, then McTominay is Jesus: Argentine great's son Diego Maradona Jr sparked debate after declaring that if Diego Maradona was "God" at Napoli, then Scott McTominay is "Jesus", hailing the Scotland midfielder as the club's second-greatest player ever following his sensational rise in Serie A.

Miami Open: Sinner breaks Novak Djokovic's record to script ATP Tour history Jannik Sinner created ATP Masters 1000 history on Monday evening at the Miami Open, registering a commanding 6-1, 6-4 win over Corentin Moutet at the Hard Rock Stadium.

Anushka Sharma earns maiden call-up as India announce squad for South Africa T20Is Anushka Sharma earned her first India call-up as the BCCI named the squad for the women's T20I series against South Africa starting April 17. Harmanpreet Kaur continued to lead the side, with Smriti Mandhana serving as her deputy.

Krunal Pandya sets sights on more IPL success with RCB: Just the beginning RCB all-rounder Krunal Pandya was instrumental in the franchise's maiden IPL triumph in 2025, delivering a string of match-winning performances. Yet, the seasoned campaigner remains hungry for more and has already set his sights on further glory.

Rinku Singh set to be appointed regional sports officer by UP government IPL: Kolkata Knight Riders' Rinku Singh is set to be named as the Regional Sports Officer by the Uttar Pradesh government. Rinku is being rewarded for his exceptional service with the Indian cricket team.

Ben Stokes breaks silence on Ashes drubbing: Hardest period of my captaincy England captain Ben Stokes has broken his silence after the team's humiliation at the hands of Australia in Ashes 2025-26. Stokes has called the series the hardest period of his captaincy journey.

MS Dhoni should be the CSK captain in IPL 2026: Aakash Chopra IPL 2026: Aakash Chopra said MS Dhoni should captain CSK if he plays IPL 2026. His comments reignited debate over Dhoni's role in the team.

AIFF women's chief seeks answers after Asian Cup blunders, kit row eruptsIPL 2026: Valanka Alemao demanded accountability after multiple lapses hit India's Asian Cup campaign. The controversy included ill-fitting jerseys and poor planning.

IPL 2026: KKR captain Ajinkya Rahane warns critics with rapid 58 in intra-squad clashIPL 2026: Ajinkya Rahane hit a rapid 58 in KKR's intra-squad clash. The knock came amid criticism over his captaincy ahead of IPL 2026.

Bumrah's words, painful lessons: Mayank Yadav's road to recovery for IPL 2026From skipping recovery sessions to surgery and back, Mayank Yadav opens up on his painful road to IPL 2026 and the conversations with Bumrah that kept him going.

Cristiano Ronaldo shares positive injury update, eases World Cup 2026 fearsCristiano Ronaldo shared a positive recovery update after his injury setback. The Portugal captain is expected to be fit for the 2026 World Cup.

Fined by Pakistan board, Sahibzada Farhan wins ICC award for T20 World Cup showOpener Sahibzada Farhan became the first Pakistan cricketer to win the ICC Player of the Month award since November 2024. Farhan scored 383 runs, including two hundreds, in the T20 World Cup 2026.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is not professional: Jitesh Sharma lifts lid on prodigyIPL 2026: Jitesh Sharma says Sooryavanshi is 'not professional' off the field. The reason? He can't stop eating ice cream at night. Cricket's brightest teen is just being a teenager.

PSL loses another star as Rajasthan Royals confirm Sam Curran's replacementIPL 2026: Dasun Shanaka replaced injured Sam Curran in Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 squad. The Sri Lankan all-rounder joined the franchise for Rs 2 crore after ditching PSL.

IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals await Mitchell Starc's NOC from Cricket AustraliaIPL 2026: Delhi Capitals are awaiting Cricket Australia's NOC for Mitchell Starc. The franchise remains hopeful he will join before their IPL 2026 opener.

Gavaskar blasts IPL overseas stars, urges teams to drop players for last-minute exitIPL 2026: Sunil Gavaskar has called on IPL franchises to drop overseas players who pull out of tournament at the last minute, saying some are taking advantage of India's tradition of hospitality.

Hardik Pandya urged to step down as Mumbai captain ahead of new IPL seasonIPL 2026: Former India opener Kris Srikkanth has urged Hardik Pandya to relinquish the captaincy, saying Suryakumar Yadav, fresh from T20 World Cup success, should lead Mumbai Indians. Hardik has led MI since returning from Gujarat Titans in 2024.

Koneru Humpy withdraws from Candidates Tournament citing security concernsIndia's star chess player Koneru Humpy has withdrawn from the FIDE Women's Candidates Tournament. Humpy's decision comes after she lashed out at the organisers over safety concerns in warn-torn West Asia.

KL Rahul will open in IPL 2026, confirms Delhi Capitals captain Axar PatelSenior batter KL Rahul will open the innings for Delhi Capitals, revealed the team management ahead of the team's IPL opener. Last season, Delhi played with an array of opening combinations, resulting in inconsistent performances.

Armed group warns foreign players to skip PSL, says Pakistan not safe for sportsAn armed group has warned overseas players to skip the 2026 edition of Pakistan Super League, citing a volatile security situation and calling Pakistan unfit for peaceful sporting activity.

Dhoni shares wholesome moment with Sarfaraz Khan's son, video goes viralA heartwarming video of MS Dhoni and Sarfaraz Khan's son has gone viral. In the video, the CSK legend can be seen playing with Sarfaraz's one-year-old son.

New Zealand Cricket director resigns after NZ20 replaces iconic Super SmashNew Zealand Cricket has backed a new NZ20 franchise league to replace the Super Smash. Hours after the decision was made official, board director Dion Nash issued his resignation.

Virat Kohli hits rapid 29, Tim David, Venkatesh Iyer bat well in RCB practiceRoyal Challengers Bengaluru held their first practice game on Sunday, March 22, ahead of the Indian Premier League 2026 season. Their batters came off the blocks quickly and batted with aggressive intent.

Petrol Shortage League: Fans troll PSL over oil crisis stepsPakistan Super League is being trolled by cricket fans after the league decided to trim down its operations. The reaction comes after PCB head Mohsin Naqvi announced that the league will held in only 2 cities this season. That too, behind closed doors.
Messi scores 901st career goal as Inter Miami rally past New YorkLionel Messi scored his 901st career goal as Inter Miami came from behind to beat New York City FC 3-2 in a thrilling MLS clash.

Real Madrid beat Atletico in pulsating derby, stay alive in LaLiga raceReal Madrid vs Atletico Madrid: Vinicius Jr scored twice as Real Madrid came from behind to beat Atletico Madrid 3-2 in a thrilling derby, keeping their LaLiga title push firmly on track.

Carlos Alcaraz stunned by world No. 36 Sebastian Korda in Miami OpenMiami Open: World no.1 Carlos Alcaraz has been stunned by United States Sebastian Korda in the third round of the Miami Open. Korda, ranked 36th in the world, bested Alcaraz in three sets.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
